$comment: Best-effort spending-cut estimates for PA. Same caveat as rescue-proposals.json: numbers are mid-range from cited sources; treat as informative, not precise. Real savings depend on implementation, ramp-up, and political will. Sources cited per row. Refresh annually against PA IFO publications and Auditor General reports.

proposals.name: Expand pension reform for new state hires
proposals.description: Acts 120 (2010) and 5 (2017) already shifted some new hires to lower-cost benefit tiers. Further reforms — fully defined-contribution for new hires, higher retirement age, lower employer match — could save more over time. Slow ramp-up since it only affects new employees.
proposals.statusDetail: Building on Act 120 of 2010 and Act 5 of 2017; not currently before the legislature
proposals.sourceDetail: PA SERS and PSERS actuarial reports; pension reform commission analyses

proposals.name: Trim Medicaid optional services
proposals.description: PA's Medicaid covers a generous list of "optional" services beyond federal minimums (adult dental, vision, some prescriptions, etc.). Tightening or means-testing optional benefits — as several states have done — preserves core coverage while reducing cost.
proposals.statusDetail: Done by other states; not currently proposed in PA
proposals.sourceDetail: KFF state Medicaid scope-of-coverage tables; PA DHS Medicaid budget

proposals.name: Sunset stale tax credits and abatements
proposals.description: PA has dozens of corporate tax credits (KOZ, KIZ, film tax credit, R&D credit, others), many of which haven't been rigorously audited in years. The IFO has flagged several as low-ROI. Sunsetting underperformers frees up revenue without raising rates.
proposals.statusDetail: Multiple sunset bills introduced over recent sessions; tax-credit reviews ongoing
proposals.sourceDetail: PA IFO tax credit reviews; legislative committee analyses

proposals.name: 5% state workforce reduction via attrition
proposals.description: PA employs roughly 70,000 in the executive branch. A 5% reduction over 2-3 years through attrition (not replacing departures, consolidating positions) saves hundreds of millions in salary + benefits without layoffs.
proposals.statusDetail: Perennial proposal; never formally pursued at scale
proposals.sourceDetail: PA Office of Administration workforce data; comparable right-sizing efforts in other states

proposals.name: Renegotiate Medicaid managed-care contracts
proposals.description: PA pays managed care organizations (MCOs) to administer Medicaid. Squeezing administrative loadings, rate increases, and pharmacy benefit manager spreads — as several states have done — captures savings without cutting member benefits.
proposals.statusDetail: Active topic; PA Auditor General has flagged MCO costs repeatedly
proposals.sourceDetail: PA Auditor General reports; KFF studies on state MCO oversight

proposals.name: Eliminate legislator discretionary spending (WAMs)
proposals.description: "Walking-around money" — discretionary funds legislators direct to specific district projects outside the formal budget process — is opaque and bypasses normal oversight. Eliminating or strictly capping it has been proposed in multiple sessions.
proposals.statusDetail: Proposed repeatedly; resisted by legislative leadership
proposals.sourceDetail: PA Auditor General reports; budget transparency advocacy
